cards.front: What is the primary function of the Network Layer?
cards.back: To enable communication between devices on different networks and to provide routing functionality
A method of transmitting data in which packets of data are transmitted independently through a network, each packet's destination address is used to route it to the correct recipient

cards.front: What is the main difference between IPv4 and IPv6 addresses?
cards.back: IPv6 addresses are longer (128 bits) and can support a much larger number of addresses, while IPv4 addresses are shorter (32 bits) and have limited addresses

cards.front: What is the purpose of an IPv4 Datagram?
cards.back: To transmit a single packet of data over the network using the Internet Protocol (IP)

cards.front: What is a key feature of IPv6 datagrams?
cards.back: They are fragmented into smaller pieces and transmitted independently, allowing for more efficient use of network bandwidth
